title: inbox_uri from actor document not SSRF-checked before Accept dispatch and DB storage
description: server.rs:480 extracts inbox_uri from the remote actor document (controlled by the follower). The SSRF guard (is_private_host) is applied only to the follower_actor URL before fetching it, not to the inbox_uri returned in the actor document itself. A malicious actor can return {"inbox": "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data"} in their actor JSON, causing Broadside to: (1) store this private URL in the followers table, and (2) dispatch the Accept POST directly to the metadata endpoint. Every subsequent delivery fan_out also hits that URL. The same is_private_host check should be applied to inbox_uri (and shared_inbox_uri) before storage and dispatch.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
